name: Devin
description: Autonomous AI software engineer from Cognition that plans and executes multi-step engineering tasks in a sandboxed cloud workspace with its own editor, shell, and browser, and delivers work as pull requests.

evidence.value: Completes well-scoped engineering tasks end-to-end as PRs; reliability is strongest on bounded tasks like migrations, bug fixes, and test backfills

task_completion_accuracy.methodology: Assessment of task completion on scoped engineering work based on vendor documentation, customer case studies, and independent user reports

evidence.value: Operates a full cloud workspace (editor, shell, browser) with native GitHub, Slack, Jira, and Linear integrations

tool_use_reliability.methodology: Review of integrated toolchain reliability across shell, browser, and VCS operations

evidence.value: Devin 2.0 introduced interactive planning: drafts an editable plan before execution and revises it as the task evolves

multi_step_planning.methodology: Evaluation of plan generation, user-editable plans, and plan adherence on long-horizon tasks

evidence.value: Persistent Knowledge base, Playbooks for repeatable procedures, and Devin Wiki auto-generated codebase documentation carry context across sessions

memory_persistence.methodology: Review of cross-session memory features (Knowledge, Playbooks, Wiki) and session snapshot persistence

evidence.value: Iterates on failing tests and build errors autonomously; can go down unproductive paths on ambiguous tasks, consuming ACUs until interrupted

error_recovery.methodology: Assessment of autonomous debugging behavior and failure-mode reports from production users

evidence.value: Supports running multiple parallel Devin sessions on independent tasks; orchestration across Devins is coarser than dedicated multi-agent frameworks

agent_collaboration.methodology: Review of parallel session capabilities and multi-Devin task delegation

evidence.value: Each session runs in an isolated, sandboxed cloud VM separate from user infrastructure; code execution never touches the local machine

tool_sandboxing.methodology: Security architecture review of isolated cloud workspace model

evidence.value: Enterprise tier offers SSO/SAML, scoped repository access via GitHub app permissions, and secrets management for credentials

access_control.methodology: Review of identity, repository scoping, and secrets handling controls

evidence.value: Autonomous web browsing and repository content processing create injection surface; mitigations exist but are not publicly detailed

prompt_injection_defense.methodology: Threat surface analysis of autonomous browsing and untrusted repo content; limited public disclosure of defenses

evidence.value: Per-session VM isolation and tenant separation; SOC 2 Type II attested infrastructure

data_isolation.methodology: Data architecture review of tenant and session isolation claims

evidence.value: Fully proprietary product and models (in-house SWE-1.5 line plus frontier models); no source code or model weights published
Session data and workspace snapshots retained in Cognition's cloud; enterprise contracts offer training opt-out and retention controls

data_retention.methodology: Review of published retention practices and enterprise data controls

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II attestation and DPA availability for enterprise customers; GDPR posture depends on contractual terms
Code is processed by Cognition's own SWE-1.5 model line and routed to third-party frontier models for some tasks

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Data flow analysis of model routing between in-house and third-party providers

evidence.value: Cloud-only service; no self-hosted or on-premises deployment, with VPC options limited to enterprise arrangements
Well-organized docs covering onboarding, ACU model, integrations, Playbooks, and enterprise administration
Full visibility into Devin's plan, editor, shell, and browser in real time; complete session timeline is replayable

execution_traceability.methodology: Review of session visibility, live workspace observation, and replay features

evidence.value: Explicit upfront plans, step-by-step narration during execution, and PR descriptions explaining changes

decision_explainability.methodology: Assessment of plan transparency and change justification quality

evidence.value: Closed-source product; limited public benchmark reproducibility and no published model details beyond blog posts
Large and growing commercial user base (~$492M ARR reported); community is customer-driven rather than open-source contributor-driven
Assign tasks from Slack, GitHub issues, Jira, or Linear; Devin 2.0 added IDE-style interface and API access

ease_of_integration.methodology: Integration surface assessment across team workflows

evidence.value: Parallel cloud sessions allow teams to fan out many tasks simultaneously without local resource constraints

scalability.methodology: Scalability assessment of parallel cloud session model

evidence.value: Core plan from $20 pay-as-you-go at $2.25/ACU (Devin 2.0, April 2025, down from $500/mo); Team $500/mo. ACU consumption varies widely by task complexity

cost_predictability.methodology: Pricing model analysis; ACU-metered billing makes per-task costs hard to forecast

evidence.value: Session dashboards, ACU usage tracking, and admin controls for team usage oversight

monitoring_capabilities.methodology: Monitoring and usage governance features assessment

evidence.value: Closed $1B+ round at $26B valuation (2026-05-27) with ~$492M ARR; acquired Windsurf 2025-07-14, signaling strong vendor viability

production_readiness.methodology: Vendor maturity and product stability assessment

code-generation.notes: Purpose-built autonomous software engineer; excels at scoped tasks like migrations, bug fixes, test coverage, and PR-sized features

data-analysis.notes: Can write and run analysis scripts in its workspace, but is optimized for software engineering rather than analytics workflows

research-assistant.notes: Browser access enables technical research and documentation digging, though it is not designed for general research synthesis

education.notes: Replayable sessions showing plan and execution can teach engineering practice, but ACU costs make it expensive for learning

best_for: Engineering teams delegating well-scoped tasks (migrations, bug fixes, test backfills) as autonomous PRs
Organizations wanting to fan out parallel coding tasks to cloud agents without local setup
Teams that assign work from Slack, Jira, or Linear and review results as pull requests
Companies preferring a fully managed, sandboxed cloud workspace over local agent execution
strengths: True end-to-end autonomy: plans, codes, tests, browses docs, and opens PRs in its own cloud workspace
Sandboxed cloud VMs isolate execution from user infrastructure
Interactive, editable plans and fully replayable session timelines provide strong traceability
Devin 2.0 pricing ($20 entry, $2.25/ACU) dramatically lowered the adoption barrier from the original $500/mo
Persistent Knowledge, Playbooks, and auto-generated Devin Wiki retain organizational context
Strong vendor trajectory: $26B valuation, ~$492M ARR, Windsurf acquisition (2025-07-14)
limitations: ACU-metered billing makes costs unpredictable, especially when the agent pursues unproductive paths
Fully proprietary stack with no self-hosted option; code must be processed in Cognition's cloud
Reliability drops on ambiguous or large unscoped tasks, requiring careful task decomposition
Prompt injection defenses for autonomous browsing are not publicly documented
Some workloads route to third-party frontier models, complicating data governance review
Output still requires human code review; unsupervised merging is not advisable

supported_models: Third-party frontier models for select tasks
programming_languages: Most major languages (Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, etc.)
metadata.pricing: Core from $20 pay-as-you-go ($2.25/ACU); Team $500/mo; Enterprise custom
metadata.company_milestones: Acquired Windsurf 2025-07-14; raised $1B+ at $26B valuation (closed 2026-05-27); ~$492M ARR
